free_text: Total and scattering cross section measurements on Rb
3-MeV pulsed and bunched(Klystron) Van de Graaff
cm thick,viewed either by two (RCA8575) photomultiplier
tubes in coincidence for low energies or by a single
lithium metal target was 300 keV thick
Transmission. Sample thickness was varied between
3.4 and 5.0 cm to produce maximum beam attenuation
Time of flight. Accelerator pulse rate, 1o**6 Hz,
pulse length, 2 nanosec. Flight path was varied between
4 (at low energy) and 7 meters (at high E). Energy
calibration was checked by transmission through an iron
absorber and by comparing the flight times of neutrons
and gammas from the lithium target.
Data were averaged over intervals corresponding to the
of channels to correspond with the time-of-flight
resolution and considering the integrated counts
obtained both with and without the sample
Natural rubidium. Total cross section data were
analyzed as if sample were monoisotopic.
